created_for: GS LinkOps AI resource database building

records.name: DIFI Digital IF Interoperability Standard v1.3.0

structured_summary.standard_name: IEEE-ISTO Std 4900-2021 Digital IF Interoperability Standard
structured_summary.interface_role: Digitized IF data and associated metadata transport over standard IP networks
version_change_notes: Resolved flow-control issues identified in v1.2.0
structured_summary.architecture_value: Separates antenna/RF digitization from modem and signal-processing functions, enabling interoperable and virtualized ground segments.

records.confidence_level: High for the standard identity, public version, IP data-plane purpose and VITA 49.2 relationship because the source is the official DIFI Consortium standards page.
pending_confirmation_fields: Supported sample formats and bit widths
records.next_action: Add DIFI version and conformance fields to antenna, digitizer, modem and baseband records; do not treat a generic DIFI claim as verified compatibility without version-specific evidence.

records.name: DIFI Product Certification and Lifecycle Evidence Model

certification_scope: Device behavior for defined use cases
process: Results submitted to Certification Working Group
termination_conditions: Product modification affecting DIFI functionality without recertification
Uncorrected failure to perform as advertised
Vendor no longer being a consortium member
public_product_examples: Keysight PathWave Vector Signal Analysis 89600 VSA

records.confidence_level: High for the official certification process, current public certification version and listed products. Product deployment suitability remains mission-specific.
records.next_action: Create a separate certification evidence object linked to each equipment version; never store certification only as a permanent boolean field.

records.name: RF-over-IP and Extended L-Band Ground Segment Transformation

legacy_architecture: Satellite-link frequencies may range from about 2.2 GHz to above 40 GHz
BUCs and BDCs translate signals to an extended L-band IF range
Public reference identifies an extended L-band range of approximately 850-2450 MHz
Analog amplifiers, switches, matrices and splitters route IF signals to modem racks
Copper-cable degradation forces processing equipment to remain near antennas
Individual modems have finite stream and transponder bandwidth capacity
structured_summary.database_implication: Ground-station capability must model the complete signal path rather than only antenna band support.

records.confidence_level: Medium-high. The architecture description is technically detailed and hosted by the DIFI Consortium, but the article is an industry whitepaper rather than an operator-specific verified station configuration.
pending_confirmation_fields: Digitizer sample rate and effective bandwidth
records.next_action: Add RF-chain topology and modernization-stage records so matching can distinguish analog IF stations, hybrid stations and fully digitized RF-over-IP sites.
